name: Rosa Parks Museum
description: Located at the site where Rosa Parks was arrested in 1955, this museum chronicles the Montgomery Bus Boycott and Civil Rights Movement. Features interactive exhibits and the original bus.

name: National Memorial for Peace and Justice
description: The nation's first memorial dedicated to victims of lynching. Created by the Equal Justice Initiative, it features 800 steel monuments representing counties where lynchings occurred.

name: Montgomery Riverwalk & Amphitheater
description: 2.5-mile paved trail along the Alabama River with scenic overlooks, fishing areas, and the Riverwalk Amphitheater hosting concerts, festivals, and community events year-round.

name: Alabama Shakespeare Festival
description: One of the world's largest Shakespeare festivals, operating year-round in a 250,000 sq ft complex in Blount Cultural Park. Features two theaters and produces 14+ shows annually.

name: Montgomery Zoo & Mann Wildlife Learning Museum
description: 40-acre zoo featuring over 500 animals from 5 continents. Includes the Mann Museum with wildlife dioramas. Train ride, splash pad, and educational programs available.

name: Hank Williams Museum
description: Dedicated to country music legend Hank Williams Sr., who grew up in Montgomery. Features his 1952 Cadillac, personal items, recordings, and memorabilia.

name: Parks & Recreation Facilities
description: Montgomery maintains 67 public parks covering 2,100+ acres. Facilities include 25 community centers, 15 public pools, 40 playgrounds, 30 tennis courts, and 20 basketball courts.

name: Dexter Avenue King Memorial Baptist Church
description: Where Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. served as pastor from 1954-1960 and organized the Montgomery Bus Boycott. National Historic Landmark and active church with guided tours.
